provenance: Standing trustworthiness claim file. Every expected value is the data contract: per-source and per-edge counts come from tools/expected_counts.json (SHA-locked by tools/expected_counts.baseline) and the two data-inventory catalogs; the INSTANCE_OF completeness figure and the cultural-store counts are the certified live topology of the two air-gapped stores. tools/verify_manifest.py independently recomputes every observed value before this expected side is read, then diffs; exit 0 with every claim matching is the trust proof. Every claim depends only on retained artifacts (the kept core docs, tools/, tools/expected_counts.json, the live stores); no claim references any iteration, audit, or phase document.

claims.description: H2 asserts the adapter coverage, embed-text, and Pipeline-2 pytest suites pass with zero failures (exit_code 0).
H3 asserts the live MaculaToken count equals the reconciled tier-A tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json sources['MACULA-Hebrew'].expected_count.

H3 asserts the live OSHB-morphology Word count equals the reconciled tier-A tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json sources['OSHB-morphology'].expected_count (verse-child word slots; nested qere correction elements are Reading nodes, not Word nodes).

H3 asserts the live STEPBible-TAHOT TaggedToken count equals the reconciled tier-A tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json sources['STEPBible-TAHOT'].expected_count (de-duplicated populated tagged words; empty-Strong Q/K and id-collision rows are excluded and preserved via the OSHB qere seam).

H3 asserts the live STEPBible-TAGNT TaggedToken count equals the reconciled tier-A tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json sources['STEPBible-TAGNT'].expected_count (the deterministic distinct tagged-word count over the frozen upstream).

H3 asserts the live STEPBible-TVTMS VersificationRule count equals the tier-A tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json sources['STEPBible-TVTMS'].expected_count (the full contracted tvtms.parsed.json artifact).

H3 asserts the live OPENBIBLE_CROSS_REF edge count equals the tier-A-aligned tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json edge_counts['OPENBIBLE_CROSS_REF'] (expected_min == expected_max), the faithful Verse.id-keyed landed count given the available TVTMS artifact (raw rows minus the idempotent-MERGE-collapse term minus the genuinely-unresolvable KJV-Hebrew shift rows, which the adapter quarantines and never fabricates).

H3 asserts the live PARALLEL_OF Verse-to-Verse edge count equals the tier-A-aligned tol-0 figure in tools/expected_counts.json edge_counts['PARALLEL_OF'] and sources['ETCBC-parallels'].expected_count (single-target rows after the Decision 3 split, with exact-duplicate directed pairs collapsed by the binding (source,target) idempotent MERGE).

H3 asserts the live INSTANCE_OF total on the complete single-pass graph. The run.py DATASETS dispatch order (Schema Decisions, single-pass contract) places every {strong}-keyed Lemma/GreekLemma producer before its consumer, so one fresh pass lands every edge and a second pass is a true no-op; this figure is the certified complete topology.
H.2 asserts the live CulturalChunk count in the air-gapped cultural store.
H.2 asserts the live HAS_CHUNK edge count equals the CulturalChunk count (1:1, zero orphan), per Cultural Schema Decision 2.
H.2 asserts the live Work node count (one Work per work_id), per Cultural Schema Decision 2.
H.2 asserts the live Doctrine node count seeded by seed_doctrine_question_nodes.
H.2 asserts the live Question node count seeded from questions.json.
H.2 asserts the live UNDER_QUESTION edge count equals the Question count (every seeded Question reachable from a Doctrine).
H.2 asserts the conciliar source fans out to exactly four distinct conciliar.* work_ids, per Cultural Schema Decision 16 (each creed is its own Work, never one shared conciliar Work).
claims.query: MATCH (w:Work) WHERE w.work_id STARTS WITH 'conciliar.' RETURN count(DISTINCT w.work_id) AS n

claims.description: H7 asserts tools/check_thresholds_immutable.py exits 0: the tools/expected_counts.json blob SHA equals the locked tools/expected_counts.baseline, or the last change is a [SCHEMA-REVISION] commit moving the baseline in the same commit.

H7 asserts tools/verify_no_deferral.py finds zero deferral markers in the retained core docs docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/SCHEMA_DECISIONS.md, and docs/CULTURAL_SCHEMA_DECISIONS.md (explicit --path so the claim depends only on retained artifacts).
H6 asserts tools/check_adapter_purity.py exits 0 over every ingest/lexical/ adapter (no subprocess, socket, httpx, requests, urllib, aiohttp, dynamic import, or non-data-private path literal).
H8 asserts tools/check_caste.py over the full history since the Phase A.1 schema-lock exits 0 (every commit has a Caste: trailer matching its changed-file set).

H5 asserts tools/snapshot_counts.py --self-test exits 0: two consecutive snapshots over identical inputs produce a byte-identical canonical payload. This is the single-pass determinism precondition the live two-pass overall_hash comparison re-exercises.

H4 asserts tools/check_vector_quality.py --self-test exits 0: the gate still rejects a degenerate constant-direction and near-collinear unit collection while admitting a healthy dispersed unit store (distinct-vector ratio >= 0.999 with the identical-gloss exception, and direction-dispersion non-degeneracy for the unit-normalized voyage-4-large model).
H7 asserts the sha256 of tools/expected_counts.json equals the value locked in its atomic SHA-256 companion tools/expected_counts.baseline.

H7 asserts docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (a retained core doc) contains zero forbidden deferral markers. The pattern is the verify_no_deferral marker class; must_be_empty (zero matches). Re-pointed onto a retained core doc so the claim depends only on retained artifacts; the pattern is unchanged and the bound is not weakened.

H.2 asserts docs/data_inventory_catalog.json contains zero procurement_required entries flagged "deadend": true (every procurement_required entry is deadend=false and compatible_with_project=true, so none is exempt from the passed-adapter rule). must_be_empty (the deadend-true marker is absent).
